items.title: RBA: Energy shock keeps inflation risks elevated – UOB
items.summary: UOB Economist Lee Sue Ann expects the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) to keep the cash rate at 4.10% through 4Q26, after a split decision hike in March driven by Iran-related energy shocks and a strong labour market.

items.title: Lagarde speech: Economy may be more-quick to adjust if inflation rises
items.summary: European Central Bank (ECB) President Christine Lagarde signaled at the ECB and its Watchers conference at Goethe University in Frankfurt during European trading hours on Wednesday that the central bank is ready for monetary policy adjustments if inflation proves stronger.

items.title: Dow Jones futures rise on US-Iran peace proposal optimism
items.summary: Dow Jones futures inch higher 0.7% to near 46,750 during European hours, ahead of the United States (US) regular market open on Wednesday. Meanwhile, S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures gains 0.6% and 0.63% to near 6,650 and 24,360, respectively, at the time of writing.

items.title: UK: Persistent price pressures challenge BoE – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank’s Chief UK Economist Sanjay Raja notes that UK inflation data broadly matched expectations, with Headline CPI at 3% and stronger Services CPI driving a firmer Core CPI outcome.

items.title: Asian stock markets extend recovery on Trump’s ceasefire proposal
items.summary: Asian equity markets extend their Tuesday’s recovery move on Wednesday amid increased efforts from United States (US) President Donald Trump to end conflicts in the Middle East with Iran.

items.title: Iran’s Zolfaghari disgraces US ceasefire attempts
items.summary: An Iranian military spokesperson has mocked United States (US) attempts at a ceasefire deal, insisting Americans were only negotiating with themselves, according to Associated Press (AP), the Guardian reported.

items.title: HUF: Hawkish NBH stance tempers easing hopes – ING
items.summary: ING economists Peter Virovacz and Zoltán Homolya note that the National Bank of Hungary (NBH) kept its base rate at 6.25% in March 2026 and shifted to a more hawkish stance after the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Japanese Yen softens despite BoJ hawkish tone
items.summary: The USD/JPY pair edges higher to around 159.00 during the early European trading hours on Wednesday. The Japanese Yen (JPY) weakens against the Greenback due to surging energy prices driven by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: Brent: Negotiation risks keep energy outlook fragile – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Michael Wan notes that Brent Oil has dropped to around US$100 as the Dollar weakened on signs of US–Iran talks, but he stresses that negotiations are likely to be very difficult.

items.title: AUD/JPY Price Forecast: Softens below 111.00, but mildly bullish tone prevails
items.summary: The AUD/JPY cross extends the decline to near 110.85 during the early European session on Wednesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) softens against the Japanese Yen (JPY) amid cooler-than-expected Australian inflation data and uncertainty surrounding US-Iran talks.

items.title: Iran says fired missiles at Israel and US forces in bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Wednesday that they had fired missiles at Israel as well as military bases hosting US forces in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.

items.title: US Dollar Index holds above 99.00 as US-Iran talks stir uncertainty
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, extends its gains for the second consecutive day, trading around 99.30 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.
